data.title: Starbucks is downgraded by TD Cowen on profit concerns
data.description:
TD Cowen lowered its rating on Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) to Hold from Buy as it lowered profit expectations for the coffee chain operator.

Analyst Andrew Charles and his team think Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) will settle into a 2026-2028 EPS base that is below a consensus expectation that does not appear to be considering labor investments central to the turnaround. The firm also sees a risk that a return to normalized same-store sales growth is delayed by "deteriorating value perceptions, historical underperformance in recessions, & increased competition."

TD Cowen's price target on Starbucks (SBUX) of $90 is based on an earnings multiple of 24X, which was noted to be in line with the five-year average off the two-year out EPS estimate.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) slipped 0.8% in premarket action to $85.31. The 52-week high is $117.46.

data.title: Eos Energy plunges after launching $75M stock offering
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) -21.6% pre-market Thursday after saying it launched a public offering of $75M of common shares, with an underwriters option to purchase up to $11.25M of additional shares.

Eos (NASDAQ:EOSE) also will make a separate private offering of $175M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030, with an option to purchase up to an additional $26.25M of convertible senior notes.

The company said it plans to use the proceeds from the stock and note offerings to repurchase its outstanding 5%/6% convertible senior PIK toggle note due 2026 in privately negotiated transactions, prepay part of the amount due under its credit agreement with CCM Denali Debt Holdings, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kohl's surpasses Q1 estimates, affirms FY outlook
Intellia slides after safety concern for Regeneron-partnered gene editing therapy
data.description:
Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) shares tumbled in the premarket on Thursday after the biotech disclosed that a patient taking part in a late-stage trial for its Regeneron (REGN)-partnered gene editing therapy Nexiguran Ziclumeran (nex-z) developed Grade 4 liver enzyme elevations.

Citing data from its Phase 3 MAGNITUDE study of nex-z for a heart disorder called ATTR-CM (transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy), the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech said the patient was asymptomatic following Grade 4 liver transaminase elevations.

The safety events categorized as Grade 3 ALT and Grade 2 AST elevations resolved with hospitalization or medical intervention, Intellia (NASDAQ:NTLA) added in a regulatory filing late Wednesday.

The company has dosed more than 200 patients with nex-z in MAGNITUDE, for which it has so far recruited about 365 patients out of its enrollment target of roughly 765 patients, expected by early 2028.

Intellia (NASDAQ:NTLA) said the experimental therapy indicated a safety profile in line with the Phase 1 study of nex-z for ATTR amyloidosis, with infusion-related reactions and asymptomatic liver transaminase elevations being some of the reported adverse events.

In June 2021, the two companies reported interim data from that trial, indicating the potential of in vivo CRISPR genome editing for the first time in humans.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada Q2 earnings beat estimate even as credit loss provision increases
data.description:
Royal Bank of Canada's (NYSE:RY) fiscal Q2 earnings exceeded the consensus estimate, even as it boosted its provision for credit losses to brace for a weaker macroeconomic outlook.

Q2 adjusted EPS of C$3.12 (US$2.26), topping the average analyst estimate of C$3.09, fell from C$3.62 in Q1 and rose from C$2.92 in last year’s Q2.

Total revenue for the quarter ended April 30, 2025, was C$15.7B (US$11.4B), in line with the consensus and down from C$16.7B in the prior quarter but up from C$14.2B a year ago.

Strong earnings growth in Personal Banking, Wealth Management and Insurance, and higher results in Commercial Banking, were partly offset by lower results in Capital Markets, the company said.

"We saw the strength of our diversified business model reflected across our largest segments in Q2, underpinned by our robust capital position, balance sheet strength and prudent, through-the-cycle approach to risk management. Importantly, in a quarter hallmarked by macroeconomic uncertainty and market volatility," said President and CEO Dave McKay.

Q2 net interest income of C$8.06B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of C$7.59B, increased from C$7.95B in Q1 and C$7.33B in Q2 2024.

Noninterest income of C$7.62B, dipped from C$8.79B in the previous quarter and rose from C$7.53B a year ago.

Preprovision pretax earnings retreated to C$6.94B from C$7.48B in the prior quarter and grew from C$5.85B in last year’s Q2.

Provision for credit losses increased to C$1.42B from C$1.05B in Q1 and C$920M in Q2 2024.

Adjusted return on equity narrowed to 14.7%, compared with 17.2% in the previous quarter and 15.5% a year ago.

Results, by segment:

  • Personal Banking adjusted net income of C$1.64B, fell 4% Q/Q and rose 16% Y/Y.

  • Commercial Banking adjusted net income of $613M dropped 23% Q/Q and increased 5% Y/Y.

  • Wealth Management adjusted net income of C$988M dipped 5% Q/Q and grew 10% Y/Y. The Q/Q decline reflected lower fee-based revenue, due to three less days in the quarter andlower fee-based client assets driven by unfavorable market conditions.

  • Capital Markets net income of C$1.20B declined 16% Q/Q and 5% Y/Y. Compared with Q1, the unit's net income dropped mainly due to lower fixed income trading across all regions and lower M&A activity, primarily in the U.S.

  • Insurance net income of C$211M fell 22% Q/Q and increased 19% Y/Y.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Best Buy updates FY outlook to incorporate tariff impact
Energy Transfer signs 20-year deal to sell LNG to Japan's Kyushu Electric
data.description:
Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET) said Thursday it agreed to sell up to 1M metric tons/year of liquefied natural gas from its Lake Charles LNG project to Japan's Kyushu Electric Power (OTCPK:KYSEY) (OTCPK:KYSEF) in a 20-year purchase contract, subject to Energy Transfer taking a positive final investment decision on the project.

The LNG will be supplied on a free-on-board basis, and the purchase price will consist of a fixed liquefaction charge and a gas supply component indexed to the Henry Hub benchmark.

The news follows Energy Transfer's (NYSE:ET) announcement in April of a heads of agreement with MidOcean Energy for ~5M tons/year of LNG production from Lake Charles LNG; the company also recently signed a SPA with an international energy company for 1M tons/year of LNG and an HOA with a German energy company for 1M tons/year of LNG.

The agreement marks Kyushu's (OTCPK:KYSEY) (OTCPK:KYSEF) first long-term LNG procurement contract from the U.S., and is expected to further diversify the company's procurement sources and enhance the stability of its LNG supply.

data.title: Best Buy trades lower after cutting guidance due to tariff assumptions
data.description:
Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) fell in early trading on Thursday after comparable sales in the U.S. fell more than expected (-0.7% vs. -0.6% consensus) in Q1 and full-year guidance was lowered.

Revenue fell 0.9% year-over-year to $8.77 billion for the quarter that ended on May 3, primarily driven by the comparable sales decline spread across the domestic and international business. From a merchandising perspective, the largest drivers of the comparable sales decline on a weighted basis were home theater, appliances and drones. Those drivers were partially offset by growth in the computing, mobile phone and tablet categories.

Best Buy's (NYSE:BBY) domestic gross profit rate increased 10 basis points to 23.5% of sales. The higher gross profit rate was primarily due to improved financial performance from the company’s services category, including its membership offerings, which was partially offset by rate pressure within the company’s Best Buy Health business and lower profit-sharing revenue from the company’s private label and co-branded credit card arrangement.

International revenue decreased 0.6% to $640 million, primarily driven by a negative foreign currency impact of approximately 450 basis points and a comparable sales decline of 0.7%.

Adjusted operating income as a percentage of revenue was 3.8% of sales, which was level with the mark from a year ago. EPS was reported at $1.15 vs. $1.09 consensus and $1.20 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Best Buy (BBY) sees FY26 revenue of $41.1 billion to $41.9 billion (midpoint $41.5 billion) vs. $41.4 billion consensus and EPS of $6.15 to $6.30 (midpoint $6.225) vs. $6.13 consensus. "Our underlying working assumptions are that tariffs stay at the current levels for the rest of the year, and there is no material change in consumer behavior from the trends we have seen in recent quarters," highlighted CFO Matt Bilunas. "As you can imagine, and based on our history, we will continue to scenario-plan and adjust with agility as the situation evolves," he added.

Shares of Best Buy (BBY) were down 2.8% in premarket action.

data.title: Auto Trader Non-GAAP EPS of 31.66p, revenue of £601.1M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Auto Trader Group plc press release (OTCPK:ATDRY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 31.66p.
  • Revenue of £601.1M (+5.3% Y/Y).
  • Core Auto Trader revenue increased 7% and operating profit before Digital Services Tax also increased 7%.
  • Retailer revenue grew at 7%, with the number of retailer forecourts increasing 2% year-on-year.
